message:
feat(fr-store): wrap FUNCTION DUMP in upstream RDB version+CRC64 envelope (br-frankenredis-r83v)

Upstream functions.c::functionDumpCommand emits a payload pinned
to RDB_VERSION (currently 11 for Redis 7.2.x) wrapped in the
canonical Redis envelope:

  [body … | u16 LE rdb_version | u64 LE crc64]

functionRestoreCommand verifies the footer via verifyDumpPayload
before invoking the body parser. Until now our FUNCTION DUMP
returned a raw FrankenRedis-private body with no footer, so any
cross-impl envelope check (or fuzzer that corrupts the trailing
bytes) immediately failed.

- fr-store::function_dump: append the 10-byte footer (version 11
  + CRC64 over body+version) before returning. The pre-existing
  EMPTY_FUNCTION_DUMP_PAYLOAD constant already had the upstream
  shape, so the empty-libraries path is unchanged.
- fr-store::function_restore: validate the footer first and emit
  upstream's 'ERR DUMP payload version or checksum are wrong'
  on mismatch; only after the gate passes does the body parser
  run on the un-wrapped body.
- Update the two existing tests that pinned the body-parser-first
  error to expect the new envelope-gate-first wording (or accept
  either via prop-assert when the metamorphic seed produces the
  empty-payload truncation case below the 10-byte minimum).

The body itself is still FrankenRedis-private; full upstream
body-format parity (RDB-style library serialization) is the
larger v8s5-style work tracked separately.

fr-store 288 passed; fr-runtime 281 passed; fr-command 656
passed; fr-conformance 184 passed; cargo clippy --tests
-D warnings clean; cargo fmt --check clean.

Slice of br-frankenredis-r83v (FUNCTION DUMP/RESTORE format).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
